fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Oklahoma CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Oklahoma - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity oklahoma, how to transfer CNA license to oklahoma, oklahoma CNA endorsement application

board.name: Oklahoma State Dept of Health
board.mailing_address:
Oklahoma State Department of Health
Health Resources Development Services
Nurse Aide Registry
P.O. Box 268815
Oklahoma City, OK 73126-8815

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Oklahoma.

steps.title: Create a Nurse Aide Registry Account
steps.description: Navigate to the Nurse Aide Registry Management System at https://apps.health.ok.gov/NurseAide/login.aspx and click "Create New Account" to establish your applicant profile if you are a new user.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log into your Nurse Aide Registry Management System account, select the appropriate application type (e.g., Initial Certification, Reciprocity), and accurately fill out all required personal, educational, and work history information.

steps.title: Upload Required Documentation
steps.description: Submit all necessary supporting documents, such as proof of training, competency exam results (if applicable), and any other required forms, directly through the Nurse Aide Registry Management System.

steps.title: Pay Application Fees (if applicable)
steps.description: If your application type requires a fee (e.g., reciprocity), process the payment online using a credit or debit card within the Nurse Aide Registry Management System. Note that initial certification for new graduates from approved programs typically has no fee.

steps.title: Schedule and Pass Competency Exam (if required)
steps.description: If you are pursuing initial certification without prior licensure, ensure you schedule and successfully pass both the written and skills portions of the Oklahoma state nurse aide competency examination.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application through your account in the Nurse Aide Registry Management System for updates or any requests for additional information from the Nurse Aide Registry.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Oklahoma CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: There is no application fee for CNA endorsement in Oklahoma. However, background check fees may apply.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in Oklahoma.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Oklahoma?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Oklahoma while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in OK.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Oklahoma.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Oklahoma
hero.dek: To apply for or renew your Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA) license in Oklahoma, you must use the Oklahoma State Department of Health (OSDH) Nurse Aide Registry Management System.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Oklahoma
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Oklahoma CNA certification.

official_links.registration_steps: To create an account, navigate to the Nurse Aide Registry Management System login page and click the "Create New Account" link. You will need to provide personal information and create a unique username and password to establish your applicant profile before proceeding with any applications.
